description: Apollo 12 Flight Journal - Day 1, part 3: Transposition, Docking and Extraction

tokens: Booster engineer reports the manuever to separation attitude is complete.
We ask the crew to change Omnis during this manuever.
We've got intermittent data dropouts as the antenna patterns change.
Distance from Earth, 2,941 nautical miles - 2,941 nautical miles [5,447 km]; velocity, 26,571 feet per second [8,099 m/s].
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: How's the ice situation on your windows now?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: We've got cr - awful bad windows.
It's a shame because it's all the water that was on them and it looks like it'll be with us for the flight.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Maybe I'll get out and clean them later.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Apollo 11 [12] is 3½ minutes away from the separation manuever.
Distance from Earth, 3,218 nautical miles [5,960 km]; velocity, 25,996 feet per second [7,924 m/s].
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Altitude at separation was about 3,800 nautical miles [7,000 km].
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Booster engineer reports the S-IVB stage nice and stable and looking good.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We can see the whole United States, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Bean reporting he could see the United States.
Command and Service modules now maneuvering into the docking attitude.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: I got an awful pretty looking Intrepid sitting out the window here, gang.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And Pete Conrad has seen the Lunar Module, the Intrepid.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Apollo 12 is in docking attitude now.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Distance from Earth now 4,500 nautical miles [8,334 km]; velocity, 23,679 feet per second [7,217 m/s].
And we are set up now in the Control Center to receive TV, scheduled for about 5 minutes from now.
We're going to pass up a message on the TV to him I think right now.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We are configured for television early if you want to punch it up.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: We're punching it up right now.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: No picture yet, still standing by.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: How does it look down there, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: This picture is coming in now.
It should be converted into color very shortly.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: How does that look to you, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I think we got it coming.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Black and white is spectacular, and the color is really pretty good, too.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: The camera is cutting off about half of Intrepid now.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: That's the best we could do.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Apollo 12 moving in to dock with the Lunar Module.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: You're looking right in the LM overhead window right now.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: And the color is really great now.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: This Dick Gordon's smooth as silk.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I think we just saw you grab it.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: We're on the way in right now.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We got a hard dock, Houston.
Both barber - I mean, both A and B are gray.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: What was that just floated past the window?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: We were in here doing the checklist.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We're seeing some little white flecks - floating past the window.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: We've got a - We're in a great big cloud of ice balls up here.
They're just all over everywhere, and there is a lot of stuff floating up out of the S-IVB itself that looks like ice or white paint chips, one of the two.?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We can even see it here.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: How does the zoom look - look to you, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It looks like it is in good position right now.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Just a second and we'll slide the camera over to the other side and give you a good Earth view.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: If you're going to leave the camera there for a few minutes more, try an F:11 stop.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: That's where we are now, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'll try a little more for you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We can see the scribe marks on the window now, on the colored TV.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: We can see the red window-sill; the ridge around the window.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Okay, we're going to move the camera now.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Distance now 6,000 [nautical] miles [11,100 km] from Earth.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We're picking up your Earth shots now.
It's still moving a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: We've got a real good view now.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Were the LM docking lights on?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: I didn't notice whether they were or or not, I had my - had my eyes glued to the Docking Target.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: How much fuel did I waste during that docking?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: That's too much; that's too much.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We're having a little trouble recognizing things here.
How about giving us a little travelog?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Well, that's the Earth you're looking at, friend.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Oh, I thought it was the Moon.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Charlie is not working again, is he?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: No, we've got him locked in a closet.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: On Apollo 11, during a television transmission while Columbia was returning from the Moon, CapCom Charlie Duke mistook an image of the Moon for Earth.
His colleagues subsequently took joy in reminding him of his error.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: You should be looking at the Yucatan Peninsula, Mexico; Baja California is in plain sight.
It's a pretty nice day down there.
The Gulf - The Western Gulf of Mexico has a cloud coverage along the coast; looks like it's almost up to Houston.
It's South and West of it.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: It looks like that garbage we came through down at the Cape is off the coast at this time.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: See, you could have waited and missed it, Dick.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Oh, I wouldn't have missed that for the world.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: That's Dick Gordon giving the description of the Earth.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Have you got your lens zoomed?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Why don't you try it backing off on it, and let us see a little bit more now?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Looks like you zoomed it in closer rather than back that time.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: The CapCom is Astronaut Jerry Carr.
Astronaut Dave Scott, back up Commander, still sitting next to Jerry at the console.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Now we can see the Earth is indeed round.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Hey, Jer, it's a fantastic sight.
The Mississippi Valley has a little bit of cloud coverage coming down from Canada, and there's some in the north - Northeast part of the country, up in the New England States.
Looks like they may be getting some snow over here in the next day or two.
Florida is cut in half by that front that went through this morning.
The West Coast looks absolutely gorgeous; Baja California is clear, looks like the San Diego/Los Angeles area to the South and West of them is a little cloud coverage- covered.
I won't say anything about smog.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: You see any more dry fronts anywhere?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Hey, that was one of the driest ones I've seen in a long time; I hope I never see another one like it.
As I look up North, there's nothing but clouds up there.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Hey, Houston, they got the CDR buried in the tunnel working while they're gawking.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Apollo 12 is 7,225 nautical miles [13,381 km] from Earth now.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Now with your zoomer, how about sliding in about halfway between where you are now and where you were before?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: funny; we see the Moon out the right window here, window number 5; looks like about one quarter.
We see the Earth out the left window.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: The crew is pressurizing the Lunar Module now.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Hey, Jer, I'm going to take the camera out of that left window out the Earth.
I got to get to work and get this thing pressurized.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Your signal strength looks a little low.
Are you on the High Gain?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Looks like the signal strength bumped up pretty well.?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: What are your plans for the TV now?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: We'll get with you in just a few minutes.
We're repressing the LM right now.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Apollo 12's distance now is 8,030 nautical miles [14,872 km].
We're at 3 hours, 43 minutes Elapsed Time into the mission.
The crew is busy now pressurizing the Lunar Module getting it ready to be moved from the adapter in which it was launched.
Looks as if they're moving the TV camera around some now.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We can see a handrail there now.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: We're back at the LM window.
We thought you were probably tired of looking at yourself.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Still a little bit of that white stuff floating up, isn't there?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Yes, there's quite a bit of it still - still around us.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We're getting the hatch out now, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: We'll go on Vox and it's yours.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We're reading you loud and clear on Vox.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: I've got Pete working up in the tunnel.
We've got the hatch - hatch is coming down between us.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Hooked on something for some reason here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And Houston, I do notice a sort of funny smell people have commented on.
It's nothing [garble] nervous, now that we expected it, but...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: How do the latches look, Pete?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm going up in the tunnel at this time.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: What did you put it on, about 7 on the VOX?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: And I don't see any bad latches so far.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, Houston, that was a real good ripple fire when they went home.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Whee, the docking probe is hot.
Oops, there's a latch that's not made, and now it is.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now go around 1 to 12 and check them all.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Well, let's go hook up some warm-up umbilicals.
Let me do a 360 up here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Don't lose him up through the...
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: How about stopping the camera down?
There is a light spot we're kind of worried about.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Yes, that was here the last flight.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: [Garble] Pete, they mean in the LM.
We can take it out of the window if you like or turn it off, Houston.
Verify extend latched engaged indicator red not Vis.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Ho, ho, ho; there's one umbilical all hooked up.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You can go ahead and turn it off if you want to.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Al Bean's reading something to you; I don't know what the hell he's reading - don't know what he's reading right now.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: You - to talk to me, you're going to have to re-set your VOX up a little - you're breaking up.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Here, let me try something, Al.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You got to put it up to about 7 or 8.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Got to hold on to this hatch.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, good boy, hang onto the hatch.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Now, let me, go after the other umbilical.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I thought you were hanging on to the hatch?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now, unhook the other umbilical and we'll be all set.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Can you turn on the tunnel lights?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Oh boy, this is so much nicer than one-g practice.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, now that looks like two umbilicals all connected to me.
And let me smoke over these latches one more time here.
I want to check the top of each one of the springs - they're all good [garble] [Pause.]
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: There's one - one latch, number 11, is a half cock - I mean a half load.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Nothing wrong with that, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: So - all the rest of them are full.
I guess before we put the hatch back up there, Dick, we want to get on the LM power?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It's on 4-D, I'll reset it, Dick.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, and I'm going to CSM.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: And it should read a half to 3 2, [garble] where we are, CSM.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We got power on the CSM, not very much.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That should go half a volt to 3.2.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Okay, Houston, you looking at that on your telemetry; does that look like we've got everything hooked up?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: There it goes up to 3 volts...
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Just hit a cycle on it.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: It looks to me like we can put the hatch back in.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: ...Hand me the old friendly hatch; I'll stick her back up in there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let's miss those hoses this trip; I think it'd make it a little easier for us.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Also, these switches would be nice to miss too.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm not in a very good position for you to hand me the hatch yet.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Remember that we are in Vox.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Now, I want to get it right; the arrow is in the wrong direction.
Rotate my way, gang, until you see an arrow, and then I'll - I'll go on up in here, and...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Which color of arrow do you want?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: The yellow's over right at me.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: All right, now, wait a minute.
Let me just cock her around here.
Now, there's a hose over on your side that's holding it, Dick.
Can you get that clear, and I'll go right up into the tunnel with it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, you ought to go up in the tunnel first, like this and then turn it.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: I'm in here - I'm in here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now, turn it; there you have it.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: There's your red one right here.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: The first thing I've got to do here, wait a minute.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I think everything's in place here, wait a minute.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now, be sure and rotate it more; you're not lined up.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: There you go, I'll buy that one right there.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: You'll buy that one right there?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Maybe just a little less than that.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Does that look like it's home to you?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: All right, let me close the vent valve [Pause.]
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And I'll turn off the lights and we're...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let's go per the checklist here, get the checklist.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Vent valve's closed, handle is latched, and I can verify that all the pieces of...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And the tunnel lights are out?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Tunnel lights are out, and this gage doesn't read Zero - it reads about plus a 10th when we get the pressure equalized.?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I need your O2fans on, and we'll watch them for you and tell you when to turn them off.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: I guess the next thing is, we want to get the surge tank and all our repress packages back up.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: What in the world is all that red stuff back there?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That's just what I was wondering; it's real pink out there.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Well, let me look and see.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: What's going on with the booster?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yes, something just - just looks like it's flowing...
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Looks like you're getting fuel vent.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Oh, no, I'll tell you what it is; the Sun is on the - on my right side...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: ...the fuel vent, and it's made a rainbow.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Can you see the apex of...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: ...of where it all comes together, out there?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Look at that, what happened there?
The vent must have shut off or something.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: There's all kinds of things going on back there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Look at all those loose objects floating along with us down there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: [Garble] there's a disaster waiting for us if we don't have those circuit breakers in over there on the LM Sep.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: All right, let's get back to business.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You want to go off Vox?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Houston, we're going to leave you on - off of Vox for a while, and we'll be back with you a little later.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: And we're standing by here for our Sep time.
What do you have for us for the Sep time?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We're looking at a Sep time of 04:13.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: That's the time the Lunar Module will be ejected from the spacecraft LM adapter; 4 hours, 13 minutes.
We're in Elapsed Time of 4 hours now, and Apollo 12's distance from Earth, 10,730 nautical miles [19,872 km].
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We will continue to stay up live.
The crew has gone off the voice operated circuit mode in which we can hear them talking back and forth to each other, however, we still are in contact with them.
We could get conversation between crew and the ground at any time so we'll stay up.
We're about 11 minutes away from LM ejection.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Houston, we're going to bring the SECS Logic On.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We've given Apollo 12 a go to arm the pyrotechnics that will cut loose the attach point of the Lunar Module and springs will eject the Lunar Module from the SLA.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: The crew now has a go for ejection of the LM.
Twelve minutes after ejection, the S-IVB will perform an evasive maneuver using its Auxiliary Propulsion System, about a 10-foot-per-second maneuver, essentially retrograde.
We'll have a slight out-of-plane component to the west.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: You do want us to Sep at 4 plus 13 plus 00.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We're having all kinds of time with this - trouble with this Mission Timer; we've had to reset that thing twice already.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Yes, the Mission Timer in the LEB is okay; it's kept good times.
So, we keep getting our little pitch fork, and I just think that we're going to have a lot of trouble with it, so we're just not going to pay much attention to it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: There goes your EMI theory, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Give you a little TV, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It looked good, and of course you still can't see anything yet; when we pitch around, I'll show it to you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We don't have our TV ground lines up at this time.
If we don't get them up in time to see the pictures, we'll record it at Goldstone and show it later.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: That was Al Bean reporting separation on time.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We're copying television now and soon as you've finished with your ejection and you're clear, we'll go ahead and enable the S-IVB evasive maneuver.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: We do have the lines back up and we are getting a picture now.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Can you see those flashes, Houston?
that's the RCS thrusters reflecting off the quads on the LM.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We're getting a black and white picture but we have not yet gotten color conversion.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: They may be a little dim for you to see.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Oh, there's the S-IVB, and I can see it venting.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: When you're well clear and you're ready for us, let us know and we'll start the maneuver to the evasive attitude.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: What's it keep venting, anyhow, Houston?
Keeps throwing out big clouds of...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We're not supposed to be venting anything.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Boy, it's throwing stuff off the sides and out the back like crazy.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Can you get us a picture?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Well, we'll get you some on the TV if we can; it just looks like it's venting something out of the rear end - big radial clouds of it coming out the back.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: As a matter of fact, Houston, I suspect that maybe you ought to enable that maneuver right now.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: ...it sure is throwing a lot of stuff out of the back of it; I ain't got any idea what it is, but it sure is throwing it out of there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I can see it firing thrusters, and I can see it starting to yaw.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Try to get it out of the center hatch window now, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We're copying your TV real clear now, and the jerking has stopped.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Earth is about one and a half times the size of a basketball right now.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let us know when that maneuver is over, please.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We'll let you know when that maneuver's complete.
Al, how big's that - how far away is that basketball.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: You probably got a better idea than I do about that one.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Could you see that thing throwing stuff out the back, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We could awhile ago, and it looks like it's got a halo around it now.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Well, that's the Sun shining in the front end.
But from the angle that we have on it, there's something venting out the - The aft engines are on either side and the upper aft engine, the engine that's away from the Earth...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: No, I'm not talking about that; I'm trying to reference it to whatever it is that's venting back there; it's a line; see that line coming out of the engine, over on the left-hand side?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We got a hunch that what happened is, when that Lox valve failed open and we tried to close it ourselves, it probably burned out that burner.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Okay and when are you going to brake the APS?
Oh, hey, it just vented something tremendously; can you see that, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We can see it now, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Houston, are you going to make that maneuver on time at 11:40?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We're looking at 426 plus 18 for that burn.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: And Houston, that's, I assume, is 11:40 after our Sep; is that correct?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Houston, we're changing the scenery on you; we'll come back to that S-IVB just before it goes.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: And that maneuver will be done at 13 minutes past Sep.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: How does the homeland look to you?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Beginning to look kind of small.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: And then there's something that's venting along the axial axis, and it's sort of taking turns.

And right now, it reminds me of some guy standing back there with a water hose just spraying it in any old direction; it's just - it keeps venting, whatever it is, and it just keeps blowing away in different directions.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'm trying to get all this on film for you.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: On your event timer; that maneuver will be 12:48.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Okay, Houston, we're going back to the S-IVB now for the burn.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Distance is 14,252 nautical miles [26,395 km] from Earth.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Are you starting the maneu - starting the Sep maneuver?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Yes, we can see it starting to move now, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: When you get a chance, turn off your O2fans.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: How much you figure you got out of that, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: That's pre-planned, did you really get that?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: If the vehicle is a shade lighter, we might have gotten just a little bit more Delta-V out of it.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Well, that thing did a fantastic job for us today.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: The sunlight is starting to come in the window and we're a little concerned about the TV, so I guess you've seen the show for today on the S-IVB and we'll look at the Earth for a little bit for you.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Roger, Dick, and we'd sure like to see what you guys look like.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Well, we look just like we did this morning when we got out of bed.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Now, there's a real reasonable guy for you.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: [Garble] we'll be glad to show you, and give me an attitude to go to; I'm not going to track that S-IVB any more.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Good attitude is Roll 58, Pitch 240, Yaw 39.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: The reason I'll leave that S-IVB, it's starting to get in line with the Sun and I can still have it but it's a little tough.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: This attitude we just fired to you is your P52 attitude for 05 plus 30.
And the stars that you can use at this attitude are number 12, Rigel; number 16, Procyon.
And the reason why we had this one in our hip pocket is that this is the same attitude that you can use for the sextant calibration after you've realigned your platform.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Give it to me again, 58 - Would you?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: And we've just turned off our TV; we've maneuvered where we no longer have the Earth or the S-IVB.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now, that was 390 in yaw, is that right, 039?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: This will save you one maneuver down the pike a ways.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: If you can give us an interior shot, I'd like to have a rough idea of when you can do it; and if you can't do it, we'll go ahead and release our TV lines.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: It's coming to you right now; you should have something there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: There's Dick Gordon with his sunglasses on.
I'll tell you, these guys weren't kidding when they said this thing shakes, rattles, and rolls when you fire the thrusters; it's like being on a jerky train.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, we're at 58 degrees in roll, 240 pitch, and 39 degrees in yaw.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We got a good picture here on our monitor.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: On the high gain, give us pitch minus 50 and yaw plus 60 and that should lock us up.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Better known to his friends as Shicky Dicky.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, Red Baron, where's your scarf?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: I think I forgot it during that boost phase.
We ought to talk to you about all that good happening.
That's a terrible way to break Al Bean into space flight, I'll tell you,
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Say, what time is the Lox blowdown on the S-IVB?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Cris says he doesn't think you guys are the same age as when you got up this morning either.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: In fact, I wish you guys would play us that DSE tape back tonight.
All Al Bean kept saying was there's power on the buses, there's power on the buses, and every light brightly inside was lit.
And I kept thinking, why is he saying that to me?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: I was saying, Al, there's so many lights on, I can't read them all to you.
He's totally recovered from launch, see that?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: That's a nice-looking hat you're wearing, Al.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: We have three of them just alike in here.
We got something else for you too.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I tell you, this Command Module's a good deal.
Dick and I, being use to the Gemini, just hand everything to Al and say "hold it." And he's got 25 things in his hands.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'm sorry I couldn't follow that S-IVB anymore, but it was really getting into the Sun, and I guess we aren't going to see much of it anyway, any more.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We'd like to have you guys start a Battery charge now.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: We are going to secure the TV too in just a minute now [garble.]
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: I think we're on Battery B in the flight plan, huh?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, we're going to stow the TV, and we'll come back at you later with something.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Slingshot burn time is 4 plus 48 and it's in attitude.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Well - have you got some gimbal angles for us - that are - well, let me ask you.
Is it going to be in our window in the attitude we're in now or not?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I kind of doubt it, Pete; do you want to watch it go?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: No, no, we'll just stay put.
We're getting hungry, and I think we're going to start getting out of these suits and eat in a little bit here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, Jerry, I'm still not too happy with the way this mission event timer is performing even though we do have the tuning fork intermittently in the window.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: I guess we'll just have to keep an eye on it.
Right now, it seems to be performing okay, but we'll watch it.
I'm not so sure that that doesn't get a glitch in it every now and then.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: And if you are going to lean a lot more heavily on your event timer, you might give us time hacks every once in a while when you're using it, and we'll set ours up and follow you down here and try to keep giving you the right kind of times.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Looks like the mission event timer in the LEB is keeping good time.
Now, let me ask you - it also - if it was central timing problem, it would also show up with the pitch fork, right?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We've never seen a pitch fork in the LEB timer, and it stayed right in Sync all the way along, so we just periodically call it 1665 and update the - the Mission Timer.
What happened is, when a pitch fork comes on, it begins to gain time on us; it keeps getting 5 or 6 seconds ahead.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: The LEB is the lower equipment bay.
The mission timer - event timer in that area is working all right - the event timer on the main display panel is not.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: The S-IVB sling shot maneuver was scheduled for 4 hours, 48 minutes Elapsed Time.
The present Elapsed Time is 4 hours, 39 minutes.
During this maneuver, the remaining liquid oxygen in the propellant tanks of the S-IVB is dumped.
Though the engine bell does provide small amount of energy, it will cause the S-IVB to go around the trailing edge of the Moon and then into a solar orbit.
This maneuver ensures that the S-IVB does not impact on the Moon or have re-contact with the Apollo 12 spacecraft.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Apollo 12's distance now at 4 hours, 40 minutes is 16,273 nautical miles [30,138 km].
Total vehicle weight now, 97,157 pounds [44,070 kg].
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: EECOM, the flight controller monitoring the electrical system, reports that the battery charge does look good.
Astronaut Buzz Aldrin has joined Jerry Carr and Dave Scott at the CapCom console.
We're about 30 seconds away from the liquid oxygen dump on the S-IVB.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We'll look for her to go by.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We've been kind of thinking here a little bit, and we'd like for you to consider a proposal here.

It's the idea of getting into the LM tonight before bedtime and going through the housekeeping portion of your checklist, short of the communications, and powering up the CMC and giving us a check and an E-Mod dump.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Yes, that sounds like a good idea.
We've been up here talking about what that launch may have done to the LM, and I think we can do that between the two P23's.
What do you think of that?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We're not going to do MCC 1, Dick.
It looks like you won't need it, so you can do that during that time when you would normally be doing MCC 1.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: We really don't have any place to, go tonight, so we don't mind working late.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: It looks like about 11 hours, and we'll work up a good solid plan for you and come up with it later
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Okay and if that was a proposal, you'd better watch the use of that language.
We won't accept those sort of things.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Should I have said this was a proposition for you?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: I snickered at those ham sandwiches this morning, but I take it all back.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: He'll be glad to hear that.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: Did you break out the jelly beans yet?
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: No, we're still reminiscing about that launch.
We was wondering why we were supposed to try SCE to Aux.
We haven't figured that one out yet.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, the reason why is because we lost all of our telemetry.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: When you went under voltage there, you lost the SCE, and we had to go Aux in order to see what happened.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: SCE is the signal conditioning equipment.
At 5 hours, 1 minute; Apollo 12 is 16,827 nautical miles [31,164 km] from Earth.
Velocity, 13,706 nautical miles [means feet per second, 4,178 m/s].
And the crew is having its meal.
We'll take this release circuit down now for a while and come back up if there's further air-ground.
This is Mission Control, Houston; 5 hours, 1 minute.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Can you explain what is going on with our Comm?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We just handed it over Goldstone to Ascension.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: What did you say about the SCE to Aux this morning on that launch?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: The reason - what happened here is we lost that dude when we went low on that Bus, so we had to have you go to Aux in order to pick it up again.
transcript.speaker: Carr

tokens: The words are - almost all of your electrical parameters come down on that SCE, and so when you go low on the Bus like that and dump it, about the only thing we can do is go to Aux and try to pick it up again.
transcript.speaker: Carr
